seo.meta_title: How to Migrate from Auth0 to Supabase Auth (2026 Guide)
seo.meta_description: Complete migration guide from Auth0 to Supabase Auth for Next.js. User export, RLS setup, cost comparison, and migration checklist.
seo.og_title: Auth0 to Supabase Auth Migration — Full Guide
seo.og_description: Migrate from Auth0 to Supabase Auth and save on pricing. Step-by-step user migration, RLS policy setup, and Next.js integration changes.

hero.headline: How to Migrate from Auth0 to Supabase Auth
hero.subheadline: Complete migration guide from Auth0 to Supabase Auth for Next.js. User export, RLS setup, cost comparison, and migration checklist.

sections.heading: Why migrate from Auth0 to Supabase Auth?
sections.body: Auth0 starts at Free – $3,000+/mo with Yes, up to 7,500 MAU with limited features. Supabase Auth starts at Free – $75/mo with Yes, 50,000 MAU included in Free tier. Beyond the cost savings, Supabase Auth includes a Postgres database with Row Level Security  –  your auth and data live in the same system. For Next.js teams already using Supabase for their database, the auth migration is a natural consolidation.

sections.heading: Export users from Auth0
sections.body: Go to Auth0 Dashboard > User Management > Users and export your user database. Auth0 supports JSON and CSV formats. Make sure to include email, user ID, and metadata fields. Auth0 doesn't export password hashes by default  –  you'll need to trigger password reset flows for existing users after the migration.

sections.heading: Set up Supabase Auth
sections.body: Create a Supabase project and configure authentication in the Dashboard. Enable the providers you were using in Auth0 (email/password, Google, GitHub, etc.). Supabase supports most common OAuth providers. Configure your site URL and redirect URLs under Authentication > Settings.

sections.heading: Import users into Supabase
sections.body: Supabase doesn't have a one-click user import from Auth0. Use the Supabase Management API or write a migration script that inserts users into the `auth.users` table. For passwordless migration, you can set a placeholder password and require users to reset on first login. Supabase's community has scripts for Auth0-to-Supabase user migration.

sections.heading: Set up RLS policies
sections.body: Move your authorization logic from Auth0 Rules and Actions to Supabase Row Level Security. RLS policies are SQL statements that run at the database level  –  they're more performant and secure than application-level checks. Write policies using `auth.uid()` to reference the authenticated user. Test each policy with both authenticated and unauthenticated queries.

sections.heading: Update Next.js middleware and components
sections.body: Replace Auth0's middleware with Supabase SSR middleware. Install `@supabase/ssr` and set up the server and browser clients. Honestly, replace Auth0's `useUser()` with Supabase's `supabase.auth.getUser()` for server components. On the client, use Supabase's `onAuthStateChange` listener to handle session events.

sections.heading: Test everything in staging
sections.body: Deploy to a staging environment and test all flows: sign-up, sign-in, social login, password reset, and protected route access. Verify RLS policies are working by attempting unauthorized data access. Check that the Supabase dashboard shows active sessions and auth logs.

sections.heading: Cut over and monitor
sections.body: When ready, update your DNS or Vercel environment variables to point to the Supabase configuration. Monitor the Supabase dashboard for auth errors, failed sign-ins, and unusual patterns. Keep Auth0 active for 1-2 weeks as a fallback. The Supabase Pro plan at $75/mo covers 50K MAU with most features included.

faq.question: Will I save money migrating Auth0 to Supabase?
faq.answer: Almost certainly. Auth0 starts at $3K+/mo for B2B features. Supabase Auth Pro covers 50K MAU at $75/mo. Even Supabase's Team plan ($599/mo) is significantly cheaper for equivalent MAU.

faq.question: Can I use both Auth0 and Supabase Auth during migration?
faq.answer: Running both in parallel is possible with feature flags. Use a feature flag to switch between auth providers. This allows gradual rollout and easy rollback if issues arise.

faq.question: Does Supabase Auth support enterprise SSO?
faq.answer: Supabase Auth supports SAML and OIDC SSO on the Team plan ($599/mo). Auth0's enterprise SSO starts at $3K+/mo. Supabase's enterprise features are more affordable but less mature.
